title: Fast Fashion's Environmental Toll
subtitle: 你的衣柜正在伤害地球

paragraphs.en: The fashion industry has become one of the world's largest polluters, driven by the fast fashion model that encourages constant consumption. Brands now release dozens of collections annually rather than traditional seasonal offerings. This acceleration produces staggering waste as consumers discard clothing after minimal use.
paragraphs.zh: 在鼓励持续消费的快时尚模式推动下，时装业已成为世界上最大的污染者之一。品牌现在每年推出数十个系列，而非传统的季节性产品。这种加速产生了惊人的浪费，因为消费者在最少的使用后就丢弃衣服。

paragraphs.en: Textile production consumes enormous resources at every stage. Growing cotton requires vast quantities of water and pesticides, while synthetic fabrics derive from petroleum. Dyeing and finishing processes release toxic chemicals into waterways, polluting communities near manufacturing facilities often located in developing countries.
paragraphs.zh: 纺织品生产在每个阶段都消耗大量资源。种植棉花需要大量的水和农药，而合成织物来源于石油。染色和整理过程将有毒化学物质释放到水道中，污染了通常位于发展中国家的制造工厂附近的社区。

paragraphs.en: Microplastic pollution from synthetic clothing represents an emerging environmental crisis. Each washing cycle releases thousands of tiny plastic fibers into water systems. These particles accumulate in oceans, entering food chains and eventually human bodies with consequences scientists are only beginning to understand.
paragraphs.zh: 合成服装的微塑料污染代表着一场新兴的环境危机。每个洗涤周期都会将成千上万的微小塑料纤维释放到水系统中。这些颗粒在海洋中积累，进入食物链并最终进入人体，其后果科学家才刚刚开始了解。

paragraphs.en: The human costs of fast fashion parallel environmental damage. Garment workers in supply chain countries often face dangerous conditions, excessive hours, and wages insufficient for basic needs. Building collapses and factory fires have killed thousands, exposing the true price of cheap clothing.
paragraphs.zh: 快时尚的人力成本与环境破坏相当。供应链国家的制衣工人经常面临危险的条件、过长的工时和不足以满足基本需求的工资。建筑倒塌和工厂火灾已造成数千人死亡，暴露了廉价服装的真正代价。

paragraphs.en: Sustainable fashion alternatives are gaining momentum among conscious consumers. Secondhand shopping, clothing rental services, and investment in quality pieces that last represent growing trends. Some brands embrace circular economy principles, designing for durability and recyclability. Individual choices to buy less and choose better can collectively transform an industry.
paragraphs.zh: 可持续时尚替代品在有意识的消费者中正获得动力。二手购物、服装租赁服务和投资于耐穿的优质服装代表着不断增长的趋势。一些品牌拥抱循环经济原则，为耐用性和可回收性而设计。少买多选的个人选择可以共同改变一个行业。
